title: Diagnose a watchdog reset or hang
description: Find why the device resets or hangs by capturing the reset cause and tracing the path that starved the watchdog kick.
body: Steps: 1. Read the reset-cause register on boot to confirm it was the watchdog, not a brownout or fault. 2. Capture the program counter / a breadcrumb log at the last kick point. 3. Trace what blocked the kick path: an ISR storm, a deadlock, a blocking call, or an infinite loop. 4. Fix the root cause — never just widen the watchdog period or disable it. 5. Re-run and confirm the watchdog no longer fires under the failing scenario. Rollback point: before any timing or priority change. Validation: reset-cause shows no watchdog resets across a soak test and the recovery path was exercised; disabling or lengthening the watchdog to stop resets hides a real hang and removes the safety net.
